ruleset.name: GURPS Lite
ruleset.license: Original mechanics references; GURPS is a registered trademark of Steve Jackson Games Incorporated. Stat-vs-3d6 roll-under mechanics are common to the generic-system family; no verbatim SJG text reproduced.
ruleset.summary: Generic universal stat-vs-3d6 roll-under system. Each attribute and skill has a target number; roll 3d6 and succeed if the total is at or under that number. Bonuses raise the target (the cap of success), penalties lower it. Critical success on a natural 3 or 4; critical failure on the target plus ten, the natural 17, or the natural 18.

Easy.description: Easy skill default — base attribute minus 4.

Average.description: Average skill default — base attribute minus 5.

Hard.description: Hard skill default — base attribute minus 6.

VeryHard.description: Very Hard skill — no default; must be learned. Base attribute minus 9 once trained at 1 point.

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.description: Muscle power and bulk. Drives HP, damage, lifting capacity, and any physical activity that depends on raw force.

attributes.name: Dexterity
attributes.description: Agility, co-ordination, and reaction speed. Underlies most active physical skills and Basic Speed.

attributes.name: Intelligence
attributes.description: Brainpower, alertness, and adaptability. Default for Will, Perception, and most mental skills.

attributes.name: Health
attributes.description: Endurance, resistance to illness and poison, and the body's reserve. Drives FP and recovery.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: Tumbling, balance, and athletic feats. Hard skill; default DX-6.

skills.name: Area Knowledge
skills.description: Familiarity with a specific region's people, places, and customs. Easy skill; default IQ.

skills.name: Brawling
skills.description: Unarmed striking. Easy skill; defaults to DX.

skills.name: Camouflage
skills.description: Conceal an object or oneself by blending into surroundings. Easy skill; default IQ-4.

skills.name: Climbing
skills.description: Scale walls, ropes, and natural features. Average skill; default DX-5.

skills.name: Driving
skills.description: Operate a ground vehicle (specify type on the character sheet). Average skill; default DX-5.

skills.name: Electronics Operation
skills.description: Use a specific category of electronic gear. Average skill; default IQ-5.

skills.name: First Aid
skills.description: Stop bleeding and stabilise injuries in the field. Easy skill; default IQ-4.

skills.name: Forensics
skills.description: Apply scientific method to crime-scene evidence. Hard skill; default IQ-6.

skills.name: Gunner
skills.description: Operate a vehicle-mounted or heavy weapon. Easy skill; default DX-4.

skills.name: Guns
skills.description: Fire portable firearms (specify category on the character sheet). Easy skill; default DX-4.

skills.name: Knife
skills.description: Wield a knife in melee or thrown. Easy skill; default DX-4.

skills.name: Lockpicking
skills.description: Open a lock without its key. Average skill; default IQ-5.

skills.name: Mechanic
skills.description: Maintain and repair a class of machine (specify on the character sheet). Average skill; default IQ-5.

skills.name: Navigation
skills.description: Find one's way using maps, instruments, or natural cues. Average skill; default IQ-5.

skills.name: Observation
skills.description: Notice details about the environment, people, or activity. Average skill; default Per-5.

skills.name: Riding
skills.description: Control a mount (specify type on the character sheet). Average skill; default DX-5.

skills.name: Shadowing
skills.description: Follow a person without being noticed. Average skill; default IQ-5.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Move quietly and remain unseen. Average skill; default DX-5.

skills.name: Streetwise
skills.description: Navigate criminal and underground social networks. Average skill; default IQ-5.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: Live off the land in a specific environment. Average skill; default Per-5.

skills.name: Swimming
skills.description: Stay afloat and move through water. Easy skill; default HT-4.

skills.name: Throwing
skills.description: Hurl an object at a target accurately. Hard skill; default DX-3.

skills.name: Tracking
skills.description: Follow a trail through outdoor or urban terrain. Average skill; default Per-5.

derivedStats.name: Hit Points
derivedStats.formula: Equal to ST at baseline; modifiable by Extra HP advantages

derivedStats.name: Fatigue Points
derivedStats.formula: Equal to HT at baseline; modifiable by Extra FP advantages

derivedStats.name: Will
derivedStats.formula: Equal to IQ at baseline; covers mental fortitude and resistance

derivedStats.name: Perception
derivedStats.formula: Equal to IQ at baseline; the sense-based attribute used for awareness rolls

derivedStats.name: Basic Move
derivedStats.formula: Basic Speed rounded down; the number of yards moved in a step

derivedStats.name: Dodge
derivedStats.formula: Basic Speed + 3, rounded down; the active defence against attacks

values.label: Healthy
values.description: Above 1/3 of HP. No injury penalties.

values.label: Reeling
values.description: Below 1/3 of HP. Move and Dodge halved (round up).

values.label: Collapse Risk
values.description: At 0 HP or below. Roll HT each turn to stay conscious.

values.label: Unconscious
values.description: Failed a consciousness check, knocked out, or HP at -1xHP.

values.label: Mortally Wounded
values.description: HP at -1xHP or worse. Roll HT to avoid death; each subsequent multiple is a fresh roll.

values.label: Dead
values.description: Failed a death check, or HP at -5xHP.

values.label: Fresh
values.description: Above 1/3 of FP. No fatigue penalties.

values.label: Tired
values.description: Below 1/3 of FP. Move, Dodge, and ST halved (round up).

values.label: Exhausted
values.description: FP at 0. Cannot do anything strenuous without losing HP instead.

values.label: Collapse
values.description: FP below 0. Unconsciousness imminent without rest.

values.label: Crouching
values.description: -2 to hit in melee; smaller target for ranged attackers.

values.label: Prone
values.description: -4 to hit; -3 to active defences. Half move only.

values.label: Stunned
values.description: Cannot defend actively this turn. Roll IQ or HT each turn to recover.

lorebook.name: GURPS Lite Rules Reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered rules reference for the GM agent running a GURPS Lite (4th Edition) campaign. Each entry surfaces only when its keys appear in recent narration or player input, so the GM gets the right rule at the right moment without inflating every prompt.

entries.name: 3d6 Roll-Under Resolution
entries.content: Every check rolls 3d6 and SUCCEEDS if the total is at or under the effective target (attribute or skill plus any modifiers). The bell curve means 10-11 is most likely, 3 and 18 are rare. MARGIN OF SUCCESS = target - rolled total; the higher the margin, the better the success (used for damage on some attacks, contests, and timing). MARGIN OF FAILURE = rolled - target; higher margin = worse result. A success by 5 is meaningfully better than a success by 1; tell the player both the result and the margin so the narration can scale.

entries.name: Critical Success and Failure
entries.content: Critical success: natural 3 or natural 4. Critical success on an effective skill of 15+ also fires on a natural 5; an effective skill of 16+ also on a natural 6. The GM rules a spectacular extra benefit. Critical failure: natural 18 always. Natural 17 is a critical failure if the effective target is 15 or less; otherwise it's just a regular failure by 17-target. Any roll of target+10 or higher is also a critical failure (so target 8 fumbles on 18; target 6 fumbles on 16+). Critical failures roll on the critical-miss table appropriate to the action — combat fumbles drop weapons, social fumbles offend, magic fumbles backlash.

entries.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries.content: Each turn pick one MANEUVER: Do Nothing, Move (full Basic Move), Change Posture, Ready (draw/load), Aim, Attack, Feint, All-Out Attack (no defense this turn, +4 to hit OR extra attack OR +2 damage), Move and Attack (-2 or skill cap at 9 whichever is worse), All-Out Defense (no attack, +2 to one active defense OR two active defenses against the same attack OR a second defense roll), Concentrate (mental task), Wait (held action), Evaluate (+1 to next attack per turn evaluating, max +3). The Maneuver chosen FIRST in a turn shapes which actions resolve later.

entries.name: Active Defenses
entries.content: When attacked, choose ONE active defense per attack: Dodge (Basic Speed + 3, rounded down), Parry (skill/2 + 3 with a weapon you've already used this turn; unarmed Parry uses Brawling or DX), Block (Shield skill / 2 + 3, only with a readied shield). Each defense applies its own modifiers — Dodge dodges everything but suffers a -2 against attacks that come from behind; Parry suffers cumulative -4 per extra parry that turn after the first (-4, -8, -12). Block can absorb most attacks but each successful block of a heavy weapon needs a HT check to keep the shield. On a successful active defense, the attack misses entirely.

entries.name: Damage and Wounds
entries.content: Damage rolls vary by weapon and attacker's ST. Thrusting attacks roll the thrust column for the attacker's ST; swinging attacks roll the swing column. The defender's Damage Resistance (DR) from armor and natural toughness subtracts before injury is calculated. Different damage types have wound multipliers: crushing x1, cutting x1.5, impaling x2, piercing x0.5/x1/x1.5/x2 depending on caliber. Below 1/3 HP = REELING (Move and Dodge halved). At 0 HP, roll HT each turn to stay conscious; reach -1xHP and roll HT to avoid death. -5xHP is automatic death. HP recovers 1/day at rest, faster with First Aid (skill / 2, capped) or Physician.

entries.name: Fatigue Points
entries.content: Fatigue Points (FP) start equal to HT. FP costs come from: extra effort (1 FP per use), running long distances (1 FP per 100 yards above Basic Move x10), spellcasting (varies by spell), and forced marches (1 FP per hour past your limit). Below 1/3 FP = TIRED: Move, Dodge, and ST halved (round up). At 0 FP, exhaustion sets in — strenuous actions cost HP instead of FP. Below 0 FP = unconsciousness imminent. FP recovers 1 per 10 minutes of rest, faster with Will rolls (specific cases). Manage FP carefully on long expeditions — running out at the wrong time is as bad as running out of HP.

entries.name: Skill Difficulty and Defaults
entries.content: Skills come in four difficulty tiers: Easy (default at attribute), Average (attribute - 1 to learn at attribute level; defaults at attribute - 4), Hard (attribute - 2 to learn at attribute level; defaults at attribute - 5), Very Hard (attribute - 3; no default — must learn at 1 point first). Defaults: untrained characters can attempt most skills at a reduced effective skill (e.g., Climbing defaults to DX-5). Some skills have NO default (Very Hard skills, most magic, technique-specific skills). Bonuses for tools, beneficial conditions, and bonus dice raise the effective skill; penalties from injury, environment, distraction lower it.
